title: Module 4 and 5 Node.js
subtitle: Test your knowledge on Node.js fundamentals, architecture, core modules, advantages, server implementation, and multi-file web servers.

questions.t: This built-in Node.js module, imported using require('http'), allows you to create an HTTP server, make requests, and handle responses.
This method of the http module creates an HTTP server instance and accepts a callback function commonly called the 'request handler' or 'request listener.'
This JavaScript notation, represented by the symbol '=>', is used to define anonymous functions more concisely and expressively.
In the callback function '(req, res) => { ... }', this parameter represents the incoming client request.
In the callback function '(req, res) => { ... }', this parameter represents the server's response.
This method is used to set the response headers and status code for an HTTP response before the response body is sent.
This HTTP status code, commonly used in res.writeHead(), indicates that a request has been successfully processed.
This method of the http.ServerResponse object is used to write data to the response body, often in chunks.
This method indicates that the response has been fully written and should be sent back to the client; when called without data, it signals the end of the response with no additional content.
This method is called on the server instance to make it start listening for incoming HTTP requests on a specified port number.
This command is used to initialize a new Node.js project and generate a package.json file.
This metadata file provides information about a Node.js project and its dependencies, including project name, version, entry point, author, and scripts.
This command, followed by a filename (e.g., 'node Hello.js'), is used to execute a JavaScript file using the Node.js runtime.
In the sample HTTP server discussed in class, this is the port number the server was configured to listen on.
This core Node.js module includes events, classes, and methods used to deal with file input/output (I/O) operations.
This core Node.js module overcomes Node's single-threaded limitation by creating child processes that share the same server port and run simultaneously.
This core Node.js module provides a way to debug code, similar to the JavaScript console in web browsers, and prints messages to stdout and stderr.
This Node.js global object specifies the name of the directory that contains the code of the currently running application.
This Node.js module allows handling of streams that contain only binary data.
Node.js was developed by this person in 2009.
This directive is used to load (import) built-in or external JavaScript modules in Node.js.
Node.js is fast and efficient because it uses a single-threaded, event-driven architecture with this type of I/O model, meaning the server never waits for an API to return data.
In res.writeHead(200, { 'Content-Type': 'text/html' }), the Content-Type value 'text/html' indicates the response body will be in this format.
Node.js applications can be scaled in these two ways: _____ Scaling and _____ Scaling.
Node.js uses this programming language for both front-end and back-end development, improving developer productivity and code reusability.
In Node.js, there are four types of streams (objects that let you read or write data continuously): Readable, Writable, Duplex, and _____.
Node.js is free to use with no license fees because it follows this software model, with many modules supported by the community.
Node.js makes it easy to develop this type of web service API that uses standard HTTP methods.
NPM stands for this, and it is used to manage project dependencies with over 50,000 bundles available to developers.
This core Node.js module is used for connecting to a DNS server and performing name resolution.

Besides __dirname, Node.js global objects include this one that specifies the filename of the code being executed, as well as 'exports' (a reference to module.exports) and 'module' (a reference to the current module).
Besides Standard JavaScript errors, System errors, and User-specific errors, Node.js applications can also experience this fourth type of error.
In Node.js, errors are handled through exceptions using this programming construct that consists of a 'try' block and a 'catch' block.
This Node.js module intercepts errors that remain unhandled, using Internal Binding and External Binding methods.
This core Node.js module includes methods to compress or decompress files.
Node.js is described as 'Lightweight and Extensible' because it is based on JavaScript, can run on both client and server side, and supports data exchange using this format.
A Node.js Multi-File Web Server splits application code across multiple files rather than placing everything in this kind of script.
In a multi-file server structure, this file is the main entry point that creates and starts the HTTP or Express server.
In a multi-file server structure, this folder defines what happens when a user visits different URLs.
In a multi-file server structure, this folder (also called controllers) processes requests, decides what to do when a route is visited — retrieve data, run calculations, or send a response.
In a multi-file server structure, this folder (also called helpers) stores reusable functions used across the project, such as formatting dates, validating emails, or generating random IDs.
In a multi-file server structure, this file stores server settings such as the port number, host name, database connection, API keys, and other app settings.
This is a popular Node.js web framework used for building web applications, commonly used in multi-file server setups.
This middleware is used for handling multipart/form-data requests (file uploads) in ExpressJS.
This type of HTTP request encoding allows sending multiple files and form data from a client to a server.
The process of sending files from a client (e.g., a web browser) to a server is known as _____.

In the file upload process, the first step is the client browser sending files via a multipart/form-data upload request. In the second step, this middleware extracts the files and reads the form data.
In a route file like home.js, this expression is used to export a function so it can be imported by server.js using require().
This is the first of four reasons multi-file structure matters: each file handles one responsibility (routing, logic, or configuration), making the codebase easier to understand.
This is the second of four reasons multi-file structure matters: fixing a bug or updating a feature means editing a small, focused file instead of hunting through hundreds of lines.
This is the third of four reasons multi-file structure matters: as the app grows, new modules can be added without bloating existing files or creating tangled code.
This is the fourth of four reasons multi-file structure matters: utility functions, middleware, or config files can be imported and used across the app or even in other projects.
This best practice for file uploads involves checking user input to prevent security vulnerabilities like XSS or SQL injection.
This best practice for file uploads involves restricting allowed file types and sizes to prevent server overload or malicious uploads.
This best practice for file uploads involves storing files in a dedicated folder with proper permissions.
In Express.js, this method is used to associate a route path (e.g., '/about') with a specific route handler file using require().
In the multi-file server example, the Express server listens on this port number.
In a single big file approach, bug fixes risk doing this to unrelated code, which is avoided in a multi-file structure where changes stay isolated.
The multi-file server analogy compares the structure to this piece of office furniture, where each drawer (file) has one job.
In Express.js, the require('express') function is called, and then this variable is created by calling express() to represent the application instance.
In a home.js route file, this Express method is used to send a response (e.g., 'Welcome to Home Page!') back to the client, instead of using res.write() and res.end().
This best practice for file uploads involves implementing handling for upload failures or invalid files to ensure the server responds gracefully.
